paper_title: Genetic Diversity of Two Successive Generations of Selection in Bighead Catfish (Clarias macrocephalus) Populations

entities.description: Variation in the genetic makeup of a population, evaluated across generations G0, G1, and G2.
The process of adapting wild plants and animals for human use, impacting genetic variety.
Selection experiments aimed at improving fish production in bighead catfish.
Random changes in allele frequencies, cited as a cause for reduced diversity in small populations.
Inter-simple sequence repeat markers used for genetic analysis and amplification.
Method used for genomic DNA extraction from fin clips.
Software used for analyzing genetic diversity parameters.
Unweighted pair-group method with arithmetic average used for dendrogram construction.
Molecular analysis of variance used to partition genetic variation.
Chemical used for preserving fin clip samples.
Raw data matrix created from scoring the presence (1) or absence (0) of ISSR bands.
Effective number of alleles in the original wild and cultured populations.
Heterozygosity values in the original generation.
Effective number of alleles in the first successive generation.
Heterozygosity value in the first successive generation.
Percentage of polymorphic loci across the four fish populations.
Percentage of total variation found within individual populations via AMOVA.
The primary field of research involving fish production and genetics.
Lead researcher and correspondence author.
Co-author of the study.
The academic organization where the research was conducted.

relations.source: Genetic Diversity of Two Successive Generations of Selection in Bighead Catfish (Clarias macrocephalus) Populations
